cards.title: Finding Home: Couple Adopts Baby Found on Subway
cards.body: A couple discovered an abandoned baby on a New York City subway in 2000 and made the life-changing decision to adopt him. Now 26 years old, their son has become a cherished family member they never expected to have.

cards.title: Keeping Your Child's Legacy Safe Through Books
cards.body: A widowed father finds healing by preserving his late wife's book collection for their son. Reading together reminds him of the bond his wife shared with their child and helps him process grief.

cards.title: The Unique Challenge of Parenting Teenagers
cards.body: Parents of A-level students face overwhelming helplessness watching their teens navigate advanced studies. Holding flashcards for complex subjects highlights the limits of parental support during critical educational years.

cards.title: Why Grandparent Bonds Matter for Family Health
cards.body: Strong grandparent relationships protect against emotional distance that can break families apart. Small, thoughtful moments today prevent the kind of loss that haunts grandparents later.

cards.title: Are Eating Disorders Contagious Among Teens?
cards.body: A teenager's question about eating disorder contagion reveals a complex answer with hopeful implications. The condition can spread through social influence, but understanding this helps parents intervene early.

cards.title: Japan Considers Shared Parenting After Divorce
cards.body: Japan's amendment to family law may finally allow shared parenting instead of sole custody following divorce. This change could transform how separated parents co-raise their children.

cards.title: Officer Catches Baby Dropped From Burning Home
cards.body: Body cam footage captured a heroic moment when a police officer caught a baby thrown from a window during a house fire. The dramatic rescue shows quick thinking saving a child's life.

cards.title: Parents' Experience Parenting During Abuse Cycles
cards.body: Intergenerational trauma and abuse cycles involve complex factors beyond simple cause-and-effect patterns. Understanding these dynamics helps parents break harmful patterns for their children's wellbeing.
